description:
His NAACP Image Award–nominated album Creole Orchestra topped JazzWeek’s 2024
charts, affirming his role as a leading contemporary jazz innovator. Beyond
the stage, he mentors emerging artists as Professor of Studio Music and Jazz
at the University of Miami Frost School of Music, shaping future generations
through scholarship and sound.
name: Windmills Music Night - Etienne Charles - Creole Soul - BLR

performer.description: Trinidad-born trumpeter, composer, and cultural storyteller Etienne Charles creates music that uncovers overlooked histories and celebrates community memory. Praised for his warm tone, layered orchestrations, and rhythmic vitality rooted in Caribbean traditions, his work bridges jazz with social narrative. Through projects such as San Juan Hill – A New York Story, Gullah Roots, and Carnival: The Sound of a People, Charles transforms archival research into immersive performance, amplifying voices often absent from mainstream history.
performer.name: Etienne Charles - Creole Soul

name: Windmills Music Night - Etienne Charles - Creole Soul - HYD

description:
Drawing on the rich musical heritage of the Silk Road, the quartet seamlessly
blends traditional sounds with contemporary electronics and improvisation to
create immersive, cinematic soundscapes that transcend borders and genres.
Through artistic residencies and collaborations with festivals and venues
worldwide, Afro Anatolian Tales continues to shape performances that evolve
organically, forging meaningful connections between cultures, places, and
audiences.
name: Windmills Music Night - Afro Anatolian Tales-BLR

performer.description: Founded by Dutch-Turkish percussionist Sjahin During, Afro Anatolian Tales is an Amsterdam-based quartet that has spent over 15 years bringing its distinctive sound to audiences around the world. With seven albums and performances in 32 countries across five continents, the ensemble has established itself as a unique voice in contemporary world music. Their acclaimed debut album, Afro Anatolian Tales, earned international recognition, including a Top 5 selection by the BBC and a five-star review from Songlines magazine.
performer.name: Afro Anatolian Tales
